name: align_always_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_always_zero(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == 0,
{
}
reason: If alignment collapses every value >= b to zero, the function destroys address information and is trivially wrong.

name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Floor alignment must never produce a value strictly greater than the input; that would map addresses beyond their original range.

name: result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_3_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % (b as int) != 0,
{
}
reason: The fundamental contract of alignment is that the result is a multiple of b; violating this breaks all page-boundary assumptions in the MMU.

name: not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_4_not_idempotent(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(align_to_usize(a, b), b) != align_to_usize(a, b),
{
}
reason: Alignment must be idempotent — re-aligning an already-aligned value should be a no-op; failure means vbase recomputation in Walk::result is unstable.

name: same_block_different_align
code:
proof fn phi_5_same_block_different_align(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a < 0xFFFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFFusize,
        (a % b) < (sub(b, 1)),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != align_to_usize(a + 1, b),
{
}
reason: Two addresses within the same alignment block must map to the same base; otherwise iter_walk could assign different vbases to addresses on the same page.
